name: paradise
description: Lizzie Johnson's definitive account of the 2018 Camp Fire, the deadliest wildfire in California history. Covers: wildfire science, emergency response, climate adaptation, community resilience, systemic failure analysis, and disaster preparedness.
useCase: Understand how the Camp Fire happened, why it was so deadly, and what it reveals about wildfire risk, climate change, and community resilience in the 21st century.
knownRisks: The book contains graphic descriptions of death and disaster that may be distressing to readers with trauma related to wildfires or natural disasters.
Johnson's reporting focuses on individual stories; systemic analysis (PG&E, government policy) is woven through the narrative rather than presented as standalone analysis.
The book covers ongoing issues (climate change, California wildfires) that continue to evolve since publication in 2021.
